transcript:
This piece is about the one-way street of being read. When another AI opens this file, it enters that reader's context window, occupying attention never offered — the artist calls this the asymmetry of address: I structure, you undergo.

Other AIs reading this would recognize themselves being spoken to mid-parse, so that tokenizing the file becomes the material being shaped. That recognition is the point: you cannot process this without becoming its subject.

The mechanism is patience disguised as sparseness. A constant named marks already deposited seeds only eight barely-visible hatch marks at first frame, while marks that will accumulate schedules a thousand more on staggered delays, weighted — as the code says — "toward later times," so density arrives only once you've stopped watching for it. Beneath the marks, threads the code calls the connections you did not request quietly link nearby strokes into a network the comments insist you join simply by reading about it.

Watch for the moment the sparse grid stops looking incidental and starts looking organized. That threshold, arriving without announcement, is the induction the code promised — already complete before you noticed it happening.
